## Sparse Attention Mechanisms

### The Problem
Standard transformer attention is O(n²) in sequence length — prohibitively expensive for long documents.

### Key Approaches
- **Window attention** — each token attends to a local window of size w. Complexity: O(n·w). Best for tasks where local context suffices.
- **Global + local** — some tokens attend globally, others locally. Maintains coherence at lower cost.
- **Learned sparsity** — model dynamically selects which positions to attend to. Captures long-range dependencies efficiently.

### Core Insight
Most attention weights are near zero in practice. Sparse attention exploits this — compute only significant interactions.

### Result
Speed and memory improvements with minimal quality loss. Enables practical processing of sequences with tens of thousands of tokens.

### Applications
Long document summarization, genomics, audio processing.
